skill.name: angular-component
skill.description: Create modern Angular standalone components using Angular v20+ patterns, including signal inputs, outputs, host bindings, content projection, and accessible templates.
skill.summary: Build Angular v20+ standalone components with signal-based APIs and accessibility-minded patterns.
skill.author: analogjs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium indicators, but review found they are false positives in Markdown documentation and TypeScript examples. The files do not define executable scripts, shell commands, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts, so the skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The reported Ruby or shell backtick matches occur in Markdown code fences, Angular examples, and TypeScript template literals. They are instructional snippets, not commands executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files are Markdown-only guidance with TypeScript and HTML snippets. No executable script, package hook, shell wrapper, or command invocation is present at these locations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is Example Component Data
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The URL at SKILL.md line 47 is an example avatar endpoint inside a computed Angular property. It does not perform a network request from the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside a TypeScript example for deriving an image source. There is no fetch call, HTTP client usage, webhook endpoint, or secret transmission nearby.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Token Matches
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The weak-cryptography alerts match ordinary Angular wording such as change detection and OnDestroy lifecycle examples. No cryptographic API or hash algorithm is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe Angular metadata and lifecycle hooks. I found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, random-token generation, password handling, or encryption code in the skill files.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The system reconnaissance alerts point to CSS/template and data-model examples, not host inspection commands. No file, process, network, or environment enumeration is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside Angular component examples. They do not invoke uname, whoami, env, directory listing, package discovery, or similar reconnaissance behavior.

content.value_statement: Modern Angular components require signal APIs, accessible templates, and current standalone patterns. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create or refactor Angular v20+ components consistently.
actual_capabilities: Creates standalone Angular component examples using v20+ defaults.
Uses signal-based input, output, model, computed, and query patterns.
Shows host bindings for classes, attributes, styles, and keyboard events.
Applies native Angular control flow syntax instead of structural directives.
Includes accessibility requirements for ARIA, keyboard navigation, and focus states.
References advanced patterns for content projection, dependency injection, and deferred components.
limitations: Does not run Angular CLI commands or install project dependencies.
Does not verify generated code against a live application build.
Focuses on components, not routing, services, testing, or full application architecture.
Assumes Angular v20+ conventions and may not fit older Angular projects.

use_cases.title: Create New UI Components
use_cases.description: Generate standalone components with signal inputs, outputs, host bindings, and accessible template structure.

use_cases.title: Modernize Existing Components
use_cases.description: Refactor older Angular component patterns toward signals, native control flow, and OnPush-friendly state.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Patterns
use_cases.description: Keep component examples consistent across content projection, queries, dependency injection, and lazy rendering.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new Angular UI component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Angular v20 standalone user card component with signal inputs for name, avatar URL, and active state. Include accessible markup and host bindings.

prompt_templates.title: Add Interaction and Outputs
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for events and keyboard behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an Angular standalone toggle component using signal inputs and outputs. Add host bindings for ARIA state, click handling, and keyboard activation.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor to Signal Patterns
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request to update older Angular code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this Angular component from decorator inputs and outputs to signal input and output APIs. Use native control flow and avoid ngClass and ngStyle.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Composed Component System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for projection and component communication.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Angular tabs component using content queries, projected tab panels, signal state, accessible roles, and current Angular v20 template syntax.

output_examples.input: Request for a standalone profile card component with name, email, and selection behavior.
output_examples.output: A component plan and implementation outline using signal inputs, a typed output event, OnPush change detection, host classes, and accessible image text.

output_examples.input: Request to modernize an older Angular button component.
output_examples.output: A refactoring summary that replaces decorator inputs, HostBinding, and ngClass with signal inputs, host metadata, direct class bindings, and keyboard support.

output_examples.input: Request for a tabs component with projected tab content.
output_examples.output: A structured component design using content queries, active tab signal state, ARIA roles, keyboard navigation, and clear parent-child communication.

best_practices: Provide the target Angular version and existing component conventions before requesting code.
Ask for accessibility behavior when the component is interactive.
Review generated examples inside your project build and test setup.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for non-Angular frameworks or legacy Angular-only syntax.
Do not request generated components without describing inputs, outputs, and user interactions.
Do not skip project-specific review for styling, testing, and dependency constraints.

faq.question: What Angular version does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Angular v20+ standalone component patterns and modern signal APIs.

faq.question: Can it create complete Angular applications?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on component patterns, templates, bindings, and component communication.

faq.question: Does it run Angular CLI commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and code examples but does not execute commands.

faq.question: Does it support accessibility requirements?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, focus states, and WCAG AA expectations.

faq.question: Can Claude Code and Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why were static security alerts dismissed?
faq.answer: They matched Markdown code fences, template literals, example URLs, and Angular lifecycle names, not executable risky behavior.
